summary: Endometriotic lesions show features indicative of significant estrogenic stimulation; they closely mimic typical proliferative phases seen during menstrual cycles influenced heavily by elevated estrogen concentrations throughout affected tissues. This aligns with their characteristic growth pattern driven primarily by hormones like estrogen which plays an essential regulatory function both normally inside uterine lining as well as abnormally outside it where such conditions arise from ectopic implantations leading towards formation of distinct yet hormonally active structures resembling those found typically only within uterus itself but now present elsewhere causing various symptoms associated clinically speaking including pain among others depending upon location extent etc.. To reiterate succinctly for clarity sake : High Estrogen Responsiveness characterizes them best amongst given options presented here today regarding what exactly defines core pathology behind development & persistence mechanisms underlying disease process known collectively termed 'endometrioma'. Agent2 concludes based largely off comprehensive review confirming earlier hypothesis about predominant role played by female sex steroid namely estradiol across all stages involved right from
